title: x86 rustc docker image fails with SIGSEGV in podman on macos aarch64
body:
### Issue Description

x86 rustc docker image fails with SIGSEGV in podman on macos aarch64:

Issue I run into:

```
➜  ~ podman run --rm --platform linux/amd64 \
    --entrypoint rustc \
    docker.io/library/rust:1.93.0-bookworm -vV
error: rustc interrupted by SIGSEGV, printing backtrace

/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/lib/librustc_driver-90863c8161c83a53.so(+0x3b0e16c)[0xffffa6f0e16c]
/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(+0x3c050)[0xffffb384a050]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin/rustc(realloc+0x41c)[0x55555558a24c]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/lib/librustc_driver-90863c8161c83a53.so(+0x5201329)[0xffffa8601329]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/lib/librustc_driver-90863c8161c83a53.so(_ZN5alloc3ffi5c_str7CString19_from_vec_unchecked17hc606dc2ced3f8b0eE+0x51)[0xffffa9732bd1]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/lib/librustc_driver-90863c8161c83a53.so(_ZN126_$LT$std..thread..thread..thread_name_string..ThreadNameString$u20$as$u20$core..convert..From$LT$alloc..string..String$GT$$GT$4from17haecf787923a8d30fE+0x49)[0xffffa9732c8f]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/lib/librustc_driver-90863c8161c83a53.so(_RNvCslwixppQVIb9_17rustc_driver_impl21install_ctrlc_handler+0x196)[0xffffa9680d5c]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/lib/librustc_driver-90863c8161c83a53.so(_RNvCslwixppQVIb9_17rustc_driver_impl4main+0x15e)[0xffffa9681630]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin/rustc(+0x33e27)[0x555555589e27]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin/rustc(+0x33e13)[0x555555589e13]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin/rustc(+0x33e09)[0x555555589e09]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/lib/librustc_driver-90863c8161c83a53.so(_ZN3std2rt19lang_start_internal17h74b643a2cc7fe3b4E+0x4a6)[0xffffa975ba66]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin/rustc(+0x74bc7)[0x5555555cabc7]
/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(+0x2724a)[0xffffb383524a]
/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6(__libc_start_main+0x85)[0xffffb3835305]
/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.93.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin/rustc(+0x74ad4)[0x5555555caad4]

note: we would appreciate a report at https://github.com/rust-lang/rust
help: you can increase rustc's stack size by setting RUST_MIN_STACK=16777216
qemu: uncaught target signal 11 (Segmentation fault) - core dumped
```


```
➜  podman version

Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.7.1
API Version:   5.7.1
Go Version:    go1.25.5
Git Commit:    f845d14e941889ba4c071f35233d09b29d363c75
Built:         Wed Dec 10 06:58:02 2025
Build Origin:  pkginstaller
OS/Arch:       darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.7.1
API Version:  5.7.1
Go Version:   go1.25.4 X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:   f845d14e941889ba4c071f35233d09b29d363c75
Built:        Mon Dec  8 16:00:00 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```





### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Run podman run --rm --platform linux/amd64 \
    --entrypoint rustc \
    docker.io/library/rust:1.93.0-bookworm -vV

Note that e.g., `podman run --rm -it --platform linux/amd64 docker.io/library/rust:1.93.0-bookworm bash`

works fine, but something about it makes the rustc binary crash in podman:

```
podman run --rm -it --platform linux/amd64 docker.io/library/rust:1.93.0-bookworm bash
root@45fd6054775f:/# uname -a
Linux 45fd6054775f 6.17.7-300.fc43.aarch64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sun Nov  2 15:33:04 UTC 2025 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

This also happens with other versions of the official rust docker image (1.91.0, 1.91.1, 1.92.0 etc.)

### Describe the results you received

SIGSEGV

### Describe the results you expected

No SIGSEGV

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I tried with different machine providers

```
[machine]
provider = "applehv" | "libkrun"
```

### Additional information

Happens every time

comments.body: note that the command runs fine in `podman version 5.2.5`

Two things: first, you need to enable Rosetta in the Podman machine for newer versions. Second, Rosetta is only supported when using AppleHV. You can read how to enable Rosetta in a Podman machine here: https://blog.podman.io/2025/08/podman-5-6-released-rosetta-status-update/

I was unable to reproduce this on macOS 26.3.1 and Podman v5.7.1 with Rosetta enabled according to the blog. Since macOS 26+ is required to fully benefit from Rosetta, and based on this information, I am closing this issue.
